Task.name: one_day_beijing_itinerary
Task.title: One Day Beijing Itinerary
Task.description: Create a 1-day Beijing trip itinerary including key sights, suggested timing, transportation between stops, and meal suggestions.

Version.title: 1-Day Beijing Highlights Itinerary (Classic First-Timer Route)
Version.starting_area: Central Beijing (hotel area; easy access by subway)
Version.pace: Moderate (full day with transfers; start early to avoid crowds)

assumptions.assumption: First-time visitor focusing on classic highlights
Using subway plus walking, with taxis/ride-hail as needed
Forbidden City tickets booked in advance; note it is closed Mondays
Weather may affect outdoor stops; backup indoor museum option included

Day.theme: Imperial core + viewpoints + hutongs + Temple of Heaven

Meal.suggested_spot: Local breakfast stall or small shop near your hotel

Notes.tip: Start early to beat crowds at major sights.
Notes.backup_option: If you prefer seated breakfast, pick a nearby cafe-style chain in Dongcheng/Chaoyang.
Notes.time_saver: Eat close to your hotel to avoid morning detours.

Stop.name: Forbidden City (Palace Museum)
Stop.what_to_do: Focus on the main halls and the central north-south axis for a first visit.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: Timed ticket required; book in advance (closed Mondays). Bring passport/ID.

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Subway to Tiananmen East or Tiananmen West, then a short walk toward the museum entrance area.

Notes.tip: Carry passport/ID for entry checks; arrive early for smoother entry.
Notes.backup_option: If tickets are unavailable or weather is poor, consider an indoor museum such as the National Museum of China or the Capital Museum.
Notes.time_saver: Prioritize the central axis highlights rather than trying to cover every courtyard.

Stop.name: Jingshan Park
Stop.what_to_do: Climb to the pavilion for one of the best panoramas over the Forbidden City.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: Park ticket may be required (small fee).
Stop.address_or_area: Jingshan Park (north of the Forbidden City)

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Head north from the Forbidden City area; walking is feasible depending on exit and crowds.

Notes.tip: Bring water; the climb is short but can feel steep in summer heat.
Notes.backup_option: If you are tired, shorten the park visit and spend more time in hutongs later.
Notes.time_saver: Go directly to the main viewpoint pavilion for the iconic photo.

Stop.name: Lunch in Shichahai/Houhai area
Stop.what_to_do: Refuel with a classic Beijing dish before exploring nearby hutongs.

Transport_Leg.estimated_time: 15-25 min (taxi) or longer by subway + walk
Transport_Leg.route_hint: Taxi/ride-hail is often simplest; subway + walking works if traffic is heavy.
Transport_Leg.cost_hint: Low to medium (depending on mode and traffic)

Meal.what_to_order: Peking duck or Beijing zhajiangmian (noodles with soybean paste)

Notes.tip: If you want Peking duck, consider reserving or choosing a well-reviewed spot nearby.
Notes.backup_option: If it is crowded, pick a smaller noodle/dumpling shop in the hutongs.
Notes.time_saver: Eat close to where you will start the hutong walk to avoid extra transfers.

Stop.name: Hutongs (Shichahai/Houhai)
Stop.what_to_do: Stroll lanes and courtyards; enjoy lakeside views around Shichahai/Houhai.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: N/A (public lanes); any paid courtyard sites optional.

Notes.tip: Keep an eye out for quiet side lanes away from the busiest lakeside strip.
Notes.backup_option: If weather turns bad, head to a nearby tea house or an indoor museum later in the day.
Notes.time_saver: Stick to a simple loop walk starting near your lunch spot.

Stop.name: Temple of Heaven
Stop.what_to_do: See the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests and enjoy the park atmosphere near sunset.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: Park entry; additional ticket may be required for main halls (varies by season/ticket type).
Stop.address_or_area: Tiantan Park / Temple of Heaven area

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Subway option: aim for Line 8 toward Tiantan East Gate area; taxi is direct but traffic-dependent.

Notes.tip: Late afternoon light is great for photos; allow time to walk between gates and the main hall.
Notes.backup_option: If it is raining, shorten the park portion and shift time to dinner or an indoor museum.
Notes.time_saver: Enter near the area that best matches the Hall of Prayer route to reduce walking.

Stop.name: Dinner in Wangfujing/Dongcheng (or nearby)
Stop.what_to_do: Choose an easy, flexible dinner area with many options after a full day.
Stop.address_or_area: Wangfujing / Dongcheng (or convenient to your hotel)

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Use subway for predictability at peak traffic; taxi is convenient if you are tired.

Meal.suggested_spot: Restaurants in Wangfujing/Dongcheng with many choices

Notes.tip: Pick a place with short waits or make a reservation if dining at peak time.
Notes.backup_option: If Wangfujing is too crowded, eat near your hotel or along a convenient subway line.
Notes.time_saver: Choose a restaurant close to the subway exit to minimize walking when tired.

Version.title: 1-Day Beijing Highlights Itinerary (Classic First-Timer Route)
Version.starting_area: Central Beijing (hotel area; easy access by subway)
Version.pace: Moderate (full day with transfers; start early to avoid crowds)

assumptions.assumption: First-time visitor focusing on classic highlights
Using subway plus walking, with taxis/ride-hail as needed
Forbidden City tickets booked in advance; note it is closed Mondays
Weather may affect outdoor stops; backup indoor museum option included

Day.theme: Imperial core + viewpoints + hutongs + Temple of Heaven

Meal.suggested_spot: Local breakfast stall or small shop near your hotel

Notes.tip: Start early to beat crowds at major sights.
Notes.backup_option: If you prefer seated breakfast, pick a nearby cafe-style chain in Dongcheng/Chaoyang.
Notes.time_saver: Eat close to your hotel to avoid morning detours.

Stop.name: Forbidden City (Palace Museum)
Stop.what_to_do: Focus on the main halls and the central north-south axis for a first visit.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: Timed ticket required; book in advance (closed Mondays). Bring passport/ID.

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Subway to Tiananmen East or Tiananmen West, then a short walk toward the museum entrance area.

Notes.tip: Carry passport/ID for entry checks; arrive early for smoother entry.
Notes.backup_option: If tickets are unavailable or weather is poor, consider an indoor museum such as the National Museum of China or the Capital Museum.
Notes.time_saver: Prioritize the central axis highlights rather than trying to cover every courtyard.

Stop.name: Jingshan Park
Stop.what_to_do: Climb to the pavilion for one of the best panoramas over the Forbidden City.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: Park ticket may be required (small fee).
Stop.address_or_area: Jingshan Park (north of the Forbidden City)

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Head north from the Forbidden City area; walking is feasible depending on exit and crowds.

Notes.tip: Bring water; the climb is short but can feel steep in summer heat.
Notes.backup_option: If you are tired, shorten the park visit and spend more time in hutongs later.
Notes.time_saver: Go directly to the main viewpoint pavilion for the iconic photo.

Stop.name: Lunch in Shichahai/Houhai area
Stop.what_to_do: Refuel with a classic Beijing dish before exploring nearby hutongs.

Transport_Leg.estimated_time: 15-25 min (taxi) or longer by subway + walk
Transport_Leg.route_hint: Taxi/ride-hail is often simplest; subway + walking works if traffic is heavy.
Transport_Leg.cost_hint: Low to medium (depending on mode and traffic)

Meal.what_to_order: Peking duck or Beijing zhajiangmian (noodles with soybean paste)

Notes.tip: If you want Peking duck, consider reserving or choosing a well-reviewed spot nearby.
Notes.backup_option: If it is crowded, pick a smaller noodle/dumpling shop in the hutongs.
Notes.time_saver: Eat close to where you will start the hutong walk to avoid extra transfers.

Stop.name: Hutongs (Shichahai/Houhai)
Stop.what_to_do: Stroll lanes and courtyards; enjoy lakeside views around Shichahai/Houhai.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: N/A (public lanes); any paid courtyard sites optional.

Notes.tip: Keep an eye out for quiet side lanes away from the busiest lakeside strip.
Notes.backup_option: If weather turns bad, head to a nearby tea house or an indoor museum later in the day.
Notes.time_saver: Stick to a simple loop walk starting near your lunch spot.

Stop.name: Temple of Heaven
Stop.what_to_do: See the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests and enjoy the park atmosphere near sunset.
Stop.tickets_or_entry: Park entry; additional ticket may be required for main halls (varies by season/ticket type).
Stop.address_or_area: Tiantan Park / Temple of Heaven area

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Subway option: aim for Line 8 toward Tiantan East Gate area; taxi is direct but traffic-dependent.

Notes.tip: Late afternoon light is great for photos; allow time to walk between gates and the main hall.
Notes.backup_option: If it is raining, shorten the park portion and shift time to dinner or an indoor museum.
Notes.time_saver: Enter near the area that best matches the Hall of Prayer route to reduce walking.

Stop.name: Dinner in Wangfujing/Dongcheng (or nearby)
Stop.what_to_do: Choose an easy, flexible dinner area with many options after a full day.
Stop.address_or_area: Wangfujing / Dongcheng (or convenient to your hotel)

Transport_Leg.route_hint: Use subway for predictability at peak traffic; taxi is convenient if you are tired.

Meal.suggested_spot: Restaurants in Wangfujing/Dongcheng with many choices

Notes.tip: Pick a place with short waits or make a reservation if dining at peak time.
Notes.backup_option: If Wangfujing is too crowded, eat near your hotel or along a convenient subway line.
Notes.time_saver: Choose a restaurant close to the subway exit to minimize walking when tired.

Day.theme: Great Wall day trip (Mutianyu) + relaxed evening in the city

children.children: {'type': 'Stop', 'title': 'Breakfast near your hotel + pick up snacks/water', 'time_start': '07:30', 'time_end': '08:15', 'duration': '45m', 'what_to_do': 'Quick breakfast and grab water/snacks for the wall.', 'tickets_or_entry': 'N/A', 'address_or_area': 'Near your hotel (central Beijing)', 'children': [{'type': 'Meal', 'meal_type': 'Breakfast', 'time': '07:30', 'suggested_spot': 'Local breakfast stall or bakery near your hotel', 'what_to_order': 'Baozi + yogurt/soy milk; bring extra water', 'budget_level': 'Budget'}, {'type': 'Notes', 'tip': 'Start early to reduce traffic to Mutianyu and avoid midday crowds.', 'backup_option': 'If you prefer sit-down, use a nearby cafe chain and keep it under 45 minutes.', 'time_saver': 'Buy bottled water and simple snacks before you leave the city.'}]}

{'type': 'Stop', 'title': 'Travel: Beijing → Mutianyu Great Wall', 'time_start': '08:15', 'time_end': '10:00', 'duration': '1h45m', 'what_to_do': 'Transfer to Mutianyu (most convenient via private car/ride-hail or prebooked shuttle/tour).', 'tickets_or_entry': 'N/A', 'address_or_area': 'Mutianyu (Huairou District)', 'children': [{'type': 'Transport_Leg', 'from': 'Central Beijing (hotel area)', 'to': 'Mutianyu Great Wall', 'mode': 'Private car/ride-hail or shuttle bus', 'estimated_time': '1.5–2h (traffic dependent)', 'route_hint': 'Early departure helps; if using a shuttle, confirm the exact pickup point/time the day before.', 'cost_hint': 'Medium to high (private car) or low to medium (shuttle)'}, {'type': 'Notes', 'tip': 'If you’re prone to motion sickness, sit up front and avoid reading on the drive.', 'backup_option': 'If traffic is heavy, shorten the wall time slightly and prioritize a single out-and-back section.', 'time_saver': 'Prebook transport (or join a morning shuttle) to avoid negotiating on the spot.'}]}

{'type': 'Stop', 'title': 'Mutianyu Great Wall', 'time_start': '10:00', 'time_end': '13:15', 'duration': '3h15m', 'what_to_do': 'Ride cable car/chairlift up, walk a scenic section, then return via toboggan (if operating) or cable car.', 'tickets_or_entry': 'Entry ticket + optional cable car/chairlift/toboggan. Bring passport/ID if required by vendor.', 'address_or_area': 'Mutianyu Great Wall Scenic Area', 'children': [{'type': 'Notes', 'tip': 'Mutianyu is a good balance of scenery and manageable crowds; wear grippy shoes.', 'backup_option': 'If visibility is poor, focus on a shorter restored stretch and enjoy the experience rather than distance.', 'time_saver': 'Use the cable car/chairlift to maximize time on the wall (save legs for walking the towers).'}]}

{'type': 'Stop', 'title': 'Lunch near Mutianyu (simple local meal)', 'time_start': '13:15', 'time_end': '14:00', 'duration': '45m', 'what_to_do': 'Quick, filling lunch before returning to Beijing.', 'tickets_or_entry': 'N/A', 'address_or_area': 'Mutianyu area restaurants', 'children': [{'type': 'Meal', 'meal_type': 'Lunch', 'time': '13:15', 'suggested_spot': 'Small restaurants at/near the Mutianyu entrance area', 'what_to_order': 'Stir-fried dishes + rice, noodles, or dumplings; keep it simple', 'budget_level': 'Budget to Mid-range'}, {'type': 'Notes', 'tip': 'Hydrate and consider an electrolyte drink—stairs on the wall can be deceptively tiring.', 'backup_option': 'If you prefer, bring packed snacks and eat more substantially back in the city.', 'time_saver': 'Choose the closest well-reviewed spot; avoid long sit-down meals to beat return traffic.'}]}

{'type': 'Stop', 'title': 'Travel: Mutianyu → Beijing (rest/reset)', 'time_start': '14:00', 'time_end': '15:45', 'duration': '1h45m', 'what_to_do': 'Return to the city; take a short break at the hotel after arrival.', 'tickets_or_entry': 'N/A', 'address_or_area': 'Back to central Beijing', 'children': [{'type': 'Transport_Leg', 'from': 'Mutianyu Great Wall', 'to': 'Central Beijing (hotel area)', 'mode': 'Private car/ride-hail or shuttle bus', 'estimated_time': '1.5–2.5h (traffic dependent)', 'route_hint': 'Weekend/holiday afternoons can be slow—plan a buffer.', 'cost_hint': 'Medium to high (private car) or low to medium (shuttle)'}, {'type': 'Notes', 'tip': 'A quick shower/rest makes the evening much more enjoyable.', 'backup_option': 'If you arrive later than planned, skip the extra stop and go straight to dinner.', 'time_saver': 'Avoid stopping at multiple scenic/tourist shops on the way back.'}]}

{'type': 'Stop', 'title': 'Evening stroll + dinner in Sanlitun (or Gulou/Andingmen area)', 'time_start': '18:30', 'time_end': '21:30', 'duration': '3h', 'what_to_do': 'Easy evening: browse shops, grab a relaxed dinner, and enjoy city nightlife vibes.', 'tickets_or_entry': 'N/A', 'address_or_area': 'Sanlitun (Chaoyang) or Gulou/Andingmen (Dongcheng)', 'children': [{'type': 'Transport_Leg', 'from': 'Hotel area', 'to': 'Sanlitun (or Gulou/Andingmen)', 'mode': 'Subway or taxi/ride-hail', 'estimated_time': '20–45 min', 'route_hint': 'Subway is usually the most predictable in the evening; taxi is convenient if tired.', 'cost_hint': 'Low (subway) to medium (taxi)'}, {'type': 'Meal', 'meal_type': 'Dinner', 'time': '19:00', 'suggested_spot': 'Sanlitun restaurant cluster (many options) or a well-reviewed spot near Gulou', 'what_to_order': 'Hot pot, roast duck, or a lighter northern-style set meal', 'budget_level': 'Mid-range (varies)'}, {'type': 'Notes', 'tip': 'After a wall day, prioritize comfort: shorter walks, fewer transfers, and an easy meal.', 'backup_option': 'If you’re exhausted, eat near your hotel and keep the night short.', 'time_saver': 'Pick dinner close to a subway station to minimize walking.'}]}

Task.name: cs_grad_school_application_todo_list_ms_phd
Task.title: Cs Grad School Application Todo List Ms Phd
Task.description: Comprehensive, timeline- and priority-organized to-do list for applying to Computer Science graduate school (MS/PhD), including research, tests, transcripts, LORs, SOP/PS, CV, writing sample/publications, portfolio/GitHub, advisor outreach (PhD), applications/fees, funding, and follow-ups.

Version.title: Comprehensive To-Do List for CS MS/PhD Graduate School Applications (Timeline + Priority)

assumptions.assumption: Timeline is relative to each program deadline; adjust dates to your target schools.
PhD applicants should include advisor outreach; MS applicants can skip or adapt it.
Testing requirements vary by program and applicant background (citizenship, prior degrees, language of instruction).
You will apply to multiple programs and reuse core materials with program-specific tailoring.

notes.note: Priority legend: P0 = deadline-critical or blocks other steps; P1 = important quality boosters; P2 = nice-to-have.
Keep a single master spreadsheet for programs, requirements, deadlines, fees, and submission status.
Submit 3-7 days before deadlines to avoid portal and payment issues.

Phase.name: Program Research and Planning
Phase.goal: Build a realistic target list and a materials/testing plan that drives the rest of the application cycle.

Workstream.name: Program Research and Target List
Workstream.scope: Identify programs, tracks, faculty fit, and requirements; define reach/match/safety distribution.

Workstream.name: Testing Plan (GRE/TOEFL/IELTS as needed)
Workstream.scope: Confirm which tests are required/recommended and plan registration, preparation, and score reporting.

Workstream.name: Budget and Logistics
Workstream.scope: Plan for fees, transcript costs, testing costs, and time allocation.

Phase.name: Testing, Recommenders, and Early Drafts
Phase.goal: Complete exams early, secure recommenders, and draft core writing materials.

Workstream.name: Testing Execution
Workstream.scope: Register, prepare, take tests, and handle score reporting.

Workstream.name: Letters of Recommendation
Workstream.scope: Select recommenders, request letters early, and provide a complete packet.

Workstream.name: Core Writing (Early Drafts)
Workstream.scope: Draft a master SOP/personal statement and collect evidence for claims.

Phase.name: Core Materials (Polish) and PhD Advisor Outreach
Phase.goal: Finalize core documents, prepare supporting artifacts, and conduct PhD outreach if applicable.

Workstream.name: Transcripts and Academic Records
Workstream.scope: Request, verify, and prepare transcript materials per school rules.

Workstream.name: Resume/CV
Workstream.scope: Create a CS-focused CV and variants as required.

Workstream.name: Writing Sample and Publications
Workstream.scope: Select, polish, and format writing samples and publication lists.

Workstream.name: Portfolio/GitHub
Workstream.scope: Curate projects and ensure repos are clean, reproducible, and easy to evaluate.

Workstream.name: PhD Advisor Outreach (If Applicable)
Workstream.scope: Identify faculty, email effectively, and prepare for calls/interviews.

Phase.name: Application Assembly and Submission
Phase.goal: Tailor materials per program, complete portals, ensure LORs and scores arrive, and submit early.

Workstream.name: Program-Specific Tailoring
Workstream.scope: Customize SOP/short answers and ensure each application is consistent and correct.

Workstream.name: Application Forms, Uploads, and Fees
Workstream.scope: Complete portals, upload documents, manage recommenders, and submit ahead of deadlines.

Phase.name: Funding and Scholarships (Parallel Track)
Phase.goal: Maximize funding options via internal and external fellowships and understand program funding norms.

Workstream.name: Funding Landscape and Deadline Tracking
Workstream.scope: Identify RA/TA/fellowships and external scholarships; track deadlines and materials.

Workstream.name: Fellowship Applications
Workstream.scope: Draft and submit fellowship materials, reusing core statements with tailored framing.

Phase.name: Post-Submission, Interviews, and Decisions
Phase.goal: Confirm completeness, prepare for interviews/visit days, and choose among offers.

Workstream.name: Application Completion and Follow-Ups
Workstream.scope: Verify receipt of all items and address missing materials quickly.

Workstream.name: Interviews and Visit Days (Especially PhD)
Workstream.scope: Prepare technical/research discussions and evaluate advisor/lab fit.

Workstream.name: Offer Comparison and Decision
Workstream.scope: Evaluate offers and respond by deadlines; handle waitlists and negotiation when appropriate.

Version.title: Comprehensive To-Do List for CS MS/PhD Graduate School Applications (Timeline + Priority)

assumptions.assumption: Timeline is relative to each program deadline; adjust dates to your target schools.
PhD applicants should include advisor outreach; MS applicants can skip or adapt it.
Testing requirements vary by program and applicant background (citizenship, prior degrees, language of instruction).
You will apply to multiple programs and reuse core materials with program-specific tailoring.

notes.note: Priority legend: P0 = deadline-critical or blocks other steps; P1 = important quality boosters; P2 = nice-to-have.
Keep a single master spreadsheet for programs, requirements, deadlines, fees, and submission status.
Submit 3-7 days before deadlines to avoid portal and payment issues.

Phase.name: Program Research and Planning
Phase.goal: Build a realistic target list and a materials/testing plan that drives the rest of the application cycle.

Workstream.name: Program Research and Target List
Workstream.scope: Identify programs, tracks, faculty fit, and requirements; define reach/match/safety distribution.

Workstream.name: Testing Plan (GRE/TOEFL/IELTS as needed)
Workstream.scope: Confirm which tests are required/recommended and plan registration, preparation, and score reporting.

Workstream.name: Budget and Logistics
Workstream.scope: Plan for fees, transcript costs, testing costs, and time allocation.

POI.title: Workstream: Application Strategy and Timeline
POI.scope: Set an end-to-end application timeline and strategy: map deadlines per program, define milestones (materials, recommenders, tests, transcripts), and establish a weekly plan to keep Phase 2–3 execution on track.

Phase.name: Testing, Recommenders, and Early Drafts
Phase.goal: Complete exams early, secure recommenders, and draft core writing materials.

Workstream.name: Testing Execution
Workstream.scope: Register, prepare, take tests, and handle score reporting.

Workstream.name: Letters of Recommendation
Workstream.scope: Select recommenders, request letters early, and provide a complete packet.

Workstream.name: Core Writing (Early Drafts)
Workstream.scope: Draft a master SOP/personal statement and collect evidence for claims.

Phase.name: Core Materials (Polish) and PhD Advisor Outreach
Phase.goal: Finalize core documents, prepare supporting artifacts, and conduct PhD outreach if applicable.

Workstream.name: Transcripts and Academic Records
Workstream.scope: Request, verify, and prepare transcript materials per school rules.

Workstream.name: Resume/CV
Workstream.scope: Create a CS-focused CV and variants as required.

Workstream.name: Writing Sample and Publications
Workstream.scope: Select, polish, and format writing samples and publication lists.

Workstream.name: Portfolio/GitHub
Workstream.scope: Curate projects and ensure repos are clean, reproducible, and easy to evaluate.

Workstream.name: PhD Advisor Outreach (If Applicable)
Workstream.scope: Identify faculty, email effectively, and prepare for calls/interviews.

Phase.name: Application Assembly and Submission
Phase.goal: Tailor materials per program, complete portals, ensure LORs and scores arrive, and submit early.

Workstream.name: Program-Specific Tailoring
Workstream.scope: Customize SOP/short answers and ensure each application is consistent and correct.

Workstream.name: Application Forms, Uploads, and Fees
Workstream.scope: Complete portals, upload documents, manage recommenders, and submit ahead of deadlines.

Phase.name: Funding and Scholarships (Parallel Track)
Phase.goal: Maximize funding options via internal and external fellowships and understand program funding norms.

Workstream.name: Funding Landscape and Deadline Tracking
Workstream.scope: Identify RA/TA/fellowships and external scholarships; track deadlines and materials.

Workstream.name: Fellowship Applications
Workstream.scope: Draft and submit fellowship materials, reusing core statements with tailored framing.

Phase.name: Post-Submission, Interviews, and Decisions
Phase.goal: Confirm completeness, prepare for interviews/visit days, and choose among offers.

Workstream.name: Application Completion and Follow-Ups
Workstream.scope: Verify receipt of all items and address missing materials quickly.

Workstream.name: Interviews and Visit Days (Especially PhD)
Workstream.scope: Prepare technical/research discussions and evaluate advisor/lab fit.

Workstream.name: Offer Comparison and Decision
Workstream.scope: Evaluate offers and respond by deadlines; handle waitlists and negotiation when appropriate.
